rationale.hitl-workflow: Developers review outputs, constrain task chunks, intervene, edit, and approve continuation.
rationale.rules-based-checks: Participants validate agent changes with tests, linters, execution, and manual checking.
rationale.ai-review: Developers use agents for PR review and agent-generated validation tests.
rationale.oversight-explanation: Developers inspect diffs, agent reasoning, outputs, and requested architecture explanations.
rationale.agent-scope-drift: Small task chunks, monitoring, interruption, and rollback bound off-intent changes.
rationale.quality-debt: Reports identify messy, unreadable, overengineered, and hard-to-integrate generated code.
rationale.provenance-auditability: Version control and diffs preserve agent-change history for review and rollback.
rationale.counterpoint: Empirical findings oppose vibe coding and fully autonomous delegation.
